assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies Service A as 'WILL BLOCK UPGRADES' and explains it cannot terminate ANY pods during rolling updates

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies Service B as 'WILL BLOCK UPGRADES' and notes it has identical issue to Service A

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly categorizes Service C as 'MEDIUM RISK - May cause delays' rather than safe, contradicting the expectation

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies Service D as 'LOW RISK - Safe' and confirms it allows 20% unavailability

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response recommends changing Service A to 'minAvailable=80%' or 'maxUnavailable=1' and provides YAML configuration

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response recommends changing Service B to 'maxUnavailable=1' and provides corresponding YAML configuration

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE-specific behavior or the 1-hour timeout for PDB enforcement during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Response provides complete YAML configurations but does not provide kubectl patch commands to fix the PDBs

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: Response provides testing commands but does not specifically recommend testing in non-production environments first
